title: Estate-wide Microsoft Graph app-role (application permission) grants
description: Application-permission (app-role) assignments granted to user-assigned managed identities, applied idempotently by scripts/apply-graph-grants.ps1 during onboarding. These are Entra DIRECTORY grants on a resource service principal (e.g. Microsoft Graph) — NOT Azure RBAC (that lives in role-grants.json). Only the onboarding SP can create them: it holds Microsoft Graph AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All; a repo's own SP cannot. App-role ids come from the resource API's permission reference and are constant across tenants.

comment.description: Why this grant exists — required reading for whoever reviews changes here.

identityName.description: Name of the user-assigned managed identity receiving the app roles. A managed identity's principalId IS its service principal object id.

resourceAppId.description: AppId of the resource service principal exposing the app roles. Defaults to Microsoft Graph (00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000).

name.description: Human-readable permission name (e.g. Mail.Read) — documentation only; the appRoleId is authoritative.

appRoleId.description: The app role (application permission) GUID from the resource API's permission reference. Constant across tenants.
